zh.Hull, Yorkshire, England, United Kingdom: 英國英格蘭越可爾赫爾
zh.lifeSummary: Margaret De Chesney於1220年出生在英國英格蘭越可爾赫爾。她於1240年與Walter Dickinsonne在英國英格蘭越可爾赫爾結婚。他們至少是2個兒子的父母。她去世在她的家鄉。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Margaret De Chesney于1220年出生在英国英格兰越可爾赫爾。她于1240年与Walter Dickinsonne在英国英格兰越可爾赫爾结婚。他们至少是2个儿子的父母。她去世在她的家乡。

nameMeanings.name: De
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Chesney
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Scottish and Irish (Antrim, Down): shortened Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac (an) tSasanaigh, see McChesney .
English (of Norman origin): variant of   Chaney  , a cognate of 3 below.
French: topographic name for someone who lived by or in an oak wood, from Old French chesnai ‘oak grove’ (from chesne ‘oak’). Compare Cheney 2.

variants.name: McChesney
meanings: Scottish (Galloway) and northern Irish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac (an) tSasanaigh ‘son of the Saxon (i.e. the Englishman)’. The spelling seems to represent Mac Shasanaigh, to be pronounced originally as McHasney, rather than the English spelling ch.

variants.name: Chestnut
meanings: Scottish (Renfrewshire and Lanarkshire) and northern Irish: altered form of Chesney , shortened from McChesney . Its assimilation to the English word chestnut is due to folk etymology, the word itself being first recorded in 1519. The surname was probably taken to Ireland at the time of the 17th-century Plantation of Ulster.
English (Norfolk): probably a variant of Chestney (see Chaney ), altered by folk etymological association with the word chestnut.

variants.name: Chesna
meanings: English and Irish: variant of   Chesney  .

nameMeanings.name: Margaret
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

variants.name: Margareta
meanings: From the Latin form of Greek Margarītēs. See Margaret .

variants.name: Margaretta
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

en.lifeSummary: Margaret De Chesney was born in 1220, in Hull, Yorkshire, England, United Kingdom. She married Walter Dickinsonne in 1240, in Hull, Yorkshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 2 sons. She died in her hometown.

fr.lifeSummary: Margaret De Chesney est née en 1220, à Hull, Everwyck, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Elle a épousé Walter Dickinsonne en 1240, à Hull, Everwyck, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils. Elle est décédée dans sa ville natale.

de.lifeSummary: Margaret De Chesney wurde 1220 in Hull, Yorkshire, England, United Kingdom geboren. Sie hat 1240 in Hull, Yorkshire, England, United Kingdom Walter Dickinsonne geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne. Sie starb in ihrem Geburtsort.

it.lifeSummary: Margaret De Chesney è nata il nel 1220 nel Hull, Yorkshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Lei sposato Walter Dickinsonne nel 1240, nel Hull, Yorkshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figli. Morì nel sua cittadina.

ja.lifeSummary: Margaret De Chesneyは1220年にイギリスイギリスヨークシュアイアハルで生まれた。1240年にイギリスイギリスヨークシュアイアハルでWalter Dickinsonneと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子の親であった。彼女は故郷で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Margaret De Chesney(은)는 1220년 영국 잉글랜드 요르크실르 훌에서 태어났다. 그녀는 1240년 영국 잉글랜드 요르크실르 훌에서 Walter Dickinsonne(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 그녀의 고향에서 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Margaret De Chesney nasceu em 1220, em Hull, Yorkshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Ela casou-se com Walter Dickinsonne em 1240, em Hull, Yorkshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos. Ela faleceu em sua cidade natal.

ru.lifeSummary: Margaret De Chesney родилась в 1220 г. в местности хулл, еркшир, Англия, Великобритания. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Walter Dickinsonne в 1240 г. в местности хулл, еркшир, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына. Она умерла в её родном городе.

es.lifeSummary: Margaret De Chesney nació en 1220, en Hull, Yorkshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Se casó con Walter Dickinsonne en 1240, en Hull, Yorkshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijos. Murió en su pueblo.
